summary: Benchmark sitcom Steve and a near-perfect case of a character overwhelming the host format.
synopsis: The suspenders-first chaos engine who turned a family sitcom into a Steve event.
editorialBlurb: Steve Urkel is not merely a sitcom Steve. He is the benchmark case for what happens when a specific kind of television nuisance becomes a cultural weather system. The voice, the gait, the gadgets, the destructive certainty: every part of the performance made Steve feel like a national category.
whyItMatters: He changed the meaning of Steve on television and remains one of the clearest examples of a character overwhelming the format built around him.
appearanceContext: Recurring neighbor who rapidly became the central force of the series and a durable TV reference point.
sourceNotes: Recurring and later central character across the full run of Family Matters.
